git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
feat(guards): block git reset --hard and git clean -f in validate-bash

The Bash guard already covered remote history rewrites (git push --force)
but not local ones. Both `git reset --hard` and `git clean -f` destroy
uncommitted or untracked work with no reflog entry to recover from, which
makes them strictly less recoverable than the force-push we already deny.

The clean pattern keys on the force flag alone rather than requiring -fd:
`git clean -f` deletes untracked files just as irreversibly, and gating on
-d also made the regex misfire on the -d inside `--dry-run`. Dry runs
(`git clean -n`) and `git reset --soft` stay allowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git log --oneline -1
reason: git push --force (use --force-with-lease if you truly need it)

command:
gh pr create --base main --head demo/ship-e2e --title "feat(guards): block git reset --hard and git clean -f in validate-bash" --body "$(cat <<'BODY'
## What changed

The `validate-bash` PreToolUse hook already denied remote history rewrites (force-pushing), but nothing stopped the **local** equivalents. Two patterns added to `DANGEROUS`:

- `git reset --hard` — discards uncommitted work, no reflog entry to recover from.
- `git clean -f` — deletes untracked files irreversibly.

Both are strictly *less* recoverable than the force-push the guard already blocks, so the omission was the wrong way round.

The clean pattern keys on the force flag alone rather than requiring `-f` **and** `-d`. `git clean -f` destroys untracked files just as permanently as `git clean -fd` does, and gating on `-d` also made the regex misfire on the `-d` inside `--dry-run`, blocking a harmless preview.

Dry runs (`git clean -n`, `git clean --dry-run`) and `git reset --soft` stay allowed.

## Tests

`pytest tests/ -q` (conda env `pici`) — **50 passed**, up from 41. Nine new parametrised cases:

- Must block: `git reset --hard HEAD~1`, `git reset --hard origin/main`, `git clean -fd`, `git clean -f`, `git clean --force -x`
- Must allow: `git reset --soft HEAD~1`, `git reset HEAD src/x.py`, `git clean -n`, `git clean --dry-run -x`

## Reviewer notes

Shipped through `/ship`, which stopped at the review gate on the first pass — the change had no changelog entry (a `CLAUDE.md` hard rule) and no test coverage for the new patterns. Both fixed before this commit; `docs/CHANGELOG.md` is new in this PR.

**Known limitation, not fixed here:** the guard matches against the raw command string, so it does not distinguish a command being *executed* from one being *quoted*. It blocked this PR's own commit because the message body mentioned force-pushing by name. Worth a follow-up (strip heredoc bodies / quoted text before matching); out of scope for this diff.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
BODY
